summary: Split Kirby copied-special code out of the oversized `ftkirbyyoshiegg` translation unit into separate Marth, Peach, and Game & Watch files, then linked Peach and Game & Watch as matching objects while keeping Marth nonmatching. The PR updated splits, symbol scoping, build configuration, headers, and item dependencies so Kirby Peach Toad and Kirby Game & Watch Chef Pan code include their new owner headers instead of `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h`.
what_changed: `config/GALE01/splits.txt` shrank `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` from text range `0x8010AA64..0x8010D740` to `0x8010AA64..0x8010B1F4` and inserted new split ranges for `ftkirbyspecialmars.c`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c`.
`configure.py` added `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` and added `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c` plus `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)`.

New source/header pairs were created for Kirby Marth copied neutral special, Kirby Peach copied special, and Kirby Game & Watch copied neutral special: `ftkirbyspecialmars.[ch]`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.[ch]`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.[ch]`.

`ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` deleted most of the previously co-located copied-special code and retained the Yoshi egg portion; the diff shows removal of declarations for Marth/Peach/Game & Watch helper functions and extern constants from the Yoshi file.
`ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` was deleted/emptied in the diff, with exported prototypes moved into the new more specific headers.
`ftkirby.c` stopped including `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` and now includes `ftkirbyspecialmars.h`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.h`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.h` where those symbols are needed.
`symbols.txt` converted several address-named globals in the affected ranges into local labels such as `@237`, `@238`, `@247`, and `@251`, reflecting constants now owned locally by the split objects.
Some item state table formatting was compacted while touching `itpeachtoad.c` and `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c`; this appears incidental rather than the main decomp change.
smart_moves: The split boundaries in `splits.txt` mirror exact contiguous text ranges: Yoshi egg ends at `0x8010B1F4`, Marth runs `0x8010B1F4..0x8010C288`, Peach runs `0x8010C288..0x8010CE5C`, and Game & Watch runs `0x8010CE5C..0x8010D740`.

The PR did not force all split files to matching status: it linked Peach and Game & Watch as matching while explicitly leaving `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` nonmatching, reducing risk while still making progress.

Headers were split by feature ownership instead of keeping item code dependent on `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h`; this made `itpeachtoad.c` and `itkirbygamewatchchefpan.c` depend on the copied-special headers that actually declare the callbacks they use.
Symbol updates changed many temporary address-style globals to local `@NNN` constants, which is consistent with constants becoming file-local after object splitting.
`ftkirbyyoshiegg.c` gained a small inline helper for Yoshi egg hurtbox setup, suggesting localized refactoring to preserve matching after removing unrelated code from the file.
The new headers preserve address comments on prototypes, making the newly split functions searchable by ROM address and easier to verify against the split ranges.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic fighter file contains several copied-special implementations, split it along exact linker text and small-data boundaries before trying to link individual matching objects.
Do not keep umbrella headers around merely because item code used to include them; move prototypes into ownership-specific headers and update item includes to the smallest relevant header.

It is acceptable and useful to land a mixed matching/nonmatching split when only some new objects are ready to link; this PR demonstrates adding matching Peach/Game & Watch while leaving Marth nonmatching.
After a source split, revisit `symbols.txt` for constants whose scope changes from global placeholder names to local compiler-generated `@NNN` labels.

For copied-special code, maintain the project naming pattern `ftKb_<CopiedFighterSpecial>_<Address>` or state callback names like `ftKb_GwSpecialN_Anim`, `ftKb_PeSpecialLw_Coll`, and `ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Phys`.
Address comments in headers are useful during staged splits because they document that the prototype set matches the new split object's text range.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special files use lowercase path names: `ftkirbyspecialmars.c`, `ftkirbyspecialpeach.c`, and `ftkirbyspecialgamewatch.c`.

Kirby copied-special symbol prefixes in this slice include `ftKb_SpecialNMs_` for Marth, `ftKb_SpecialNPe_` / `ftKb_PeSpecial...` for Peach, and `ftKb_SpecialNGw_` / `ftKb_GwSpecial...` for Game & Watch.
Unidentified helper functions remain address-based as `fn_8010....`, for example `fn_8010CE5C`, `fn_8010CFB0`, `fn_8010C288`, and `fn_8010B1F4`.
Header guards in the new files use the first address in the object range, e.g. `GALE01_10B1F4`, `GALE01_10C288`, and `GALE01_10D074`.
Motion callback names follow the fighter-state suffix convention: `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll` for ground and air variants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used split-file text ranges in `splits.txt` to carve one large object into four objects without moving surrounding Donkey Kong code.
Added `.sdata` and `.sdata2` subranges for the new objects, including Yoshi egg `.sdata start:0x804D3DE8 end:0x804D3E00` and separate `.sdata2` ranges per copied-special object.
Marked only verified objects as `Object(Matching, ...)` in `configure.py` and left uncertain Marth code as `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.
Preserved stack-sensitive constructs visible in the diff such as `PAD_STACK(...)`, nested scopes, and small inline helpers, which are common tactics for matching CodeWarrior output.
Converted formerly global address-named float/double symbols in affected `.sdata2` regions into local `@NNN` labels with explicit size and alignment metadata.

Adjusted an unrelated-looking `itmewtwoshadowball.c` data split start from `0x803F7760` to `0x803F7880`, likely to keep data ranges aligned after symbol/split corrections; the diff does not provide further rationale.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text beyond the title was: "Also split `ftkirbyspecialmars`".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert `ftkirbyspecialmars.c` from NonMatching to Matching.
Search for follow-up renames of address-based helpers like `fn_8010CE5C`, `fn_8010CFB0`, `fn_8010C288`, and `fn_8010B1F4`.
Check whether `ftkirbyyoshiegg.h` was removed entirely from the repository or replaced by another Yoshi-specific header in later cleanup.
Review later symbol-map cleanups around `.sdata2:0x804D9580..0x804D95B0` for stable names for Kirby copied-special constants.
Search for similar copied-special split patterns in `ftkirbyspecialdonkey`, `ftkirbyspecialpikachu`, `ftkirbyspecialzelda`, and `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo`.
